flashcards.back: Something you can measure = a number + a unit. Example: 5 meters, 10 kg, 20 seconds.
A physical quantity that is just a number. No direction needed. Mass, temperature, time are scalars.
A physical quantity that has a number AND a direction. Velocity, force, acceleration are vectors.
Speed = how fast (60 km/h). Velocity = how fast + which way (60 km/h north). Velocity is a vector.
Without units, a number is meaningless. Is it 10 meters or 10 millimeters? Huge difference!
